channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Columbia University says it expelled some students who seized building last year
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Columbia University has expelled or suspended some students who took over a campus building during pro-Palestinian protests last spring and temporarily revoked the diplomas of others who have since graduated, officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Schumer to support GOP funding bill, unwilling to risk government shutdown as deadline nears
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senate Democratic leader Chuck Schumer relented Thursday rather than risk a government shutdown, announcing he's ready to start the process of considering a Republican-led government funding bill that has fiercely divided Democrats under pressure to impose limits on the Trump admin
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Schumer says he will vote to advance GOP spending bill, lowering threat of shutdown
item.description: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (N.Y.) announced on the Senate floor Thursday that he plans to vote to advance a House-passed six-month government funding bill, despite the strong opposition of many Democrats who say the bill would create a “slush fund” for President Trump and Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Jewish protesters flood Trump Tower's lobby to demand Mahmoud Khalil's release
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Demonstrators from a Jewish group filled the lobby of Trump Tower on Thursday to denounce the immigration arrest of Mahmoud Khalil, a pro-Palestinian activist who helped lead protests against Israel at Columbia University.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Some universities are freezing hiring and laying off staff as Trump cuts federal funding
item.description: Universities across the U.S. have announced hiring freezes, citing new financial uncertainty as the Trump administration threatens a range of cuts to federal contracts and research grants. Some have announced layoffs.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FEMA sued over hold on funds for upgrading nation's emergency alert system
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal government's steward of funding for public broadcasting stations sued the Trump administration on Thursday over its pause in grant payments for upgrading the nation’s emergency alert system.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Mexicans searching for missing relatives uncover possible mass killing site in Jalisco state
item.description: TEUCHITLAN, Mexico (AP) — When a group of citizens searching for missing relatives in the western state of Jalisco arrived at a remote ranch outside Mexico's second-largest city last week on an anonymous tip, all they had to do was push open the unlocked gate.
item.dc:creator: DIANA DE LA MORA and FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Farmers sue Trump administration over halted IRA grants
item.description: Farmers and environmental groups are suing the Trump administration over its decision to pause grants that are part of the Democrats’ climate, tax and healthcare law.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Columbine victim's death from health problems related to 1999 school shooting ruled a homicide
item.description: The death of a woman who was partially paralyzed in the Columbine High School shooting has been ruled a homicide, raising the death toll of the 1999 attack to 14.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Man describes cruelty during his two decades of captivity at his family home in Connecticut
item.description: A man rescued from a burning home in Connecticut told a shocking story of cruelty and constant hunger as he was held captive in a single room for 20 years by his father and stepmother, according to a newly released arrest warrant.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Universal Orlando opens ticket sales for Epic Universe, set to debut in May
item.description: Universal Orlando Resort has opened ticket sales for its newest theme park, Universal Epic Universe, ahead of its scheduled grand opening on May 22, 2025. Starting today, visitors can purchase general admission tickets, including single-day and multi-day options, for visits beginning in June 2025.
item.dc:creator: Brody Wooddell, KTLA 5

item.title: March megastorm may bring blizzards, tornadoes, flooding and even fires across much of US
item.description: More than 100 million people in the U.S. are in the path of an expected massive March storm starting Friday.
item.dc:creator: Isabella O'Malley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Parents arrested after refusing child a ride to school; found walking on highway
item.description: The Midland Police Department arrested two people earlier this month after a young girl was found walking alone on a busy street in the early morning hours.
item.dc:creator: Gabriella Meza, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration asks Supreme Court to partly allow birthright citizenship restrictions
item.description: The Trump administration is asking the Supreme Court to allow restrictions on birthright citizenship to partly take effect while legal fights play out.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Khalil protestors fill up Trump Tower
item.description: Protesters opposed to the detention of Palestinian activist Mahmoud Khalil filled up Trump Tower in New York on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KRON4

item.title: Oklahoma lawmakers push to eradicate invasive red cedar trees amid fire danger
item.description: "The oils and the resins that are in the trees make them just nearly explosive in the right conditions," said Rep. Dobrinski.
item.dc:creator: Sydnee Batzlaff/KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: The Scopes monkey trial took place 100 years ago, but the fight isn't over
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — They called it the “monkey trial.” It was supposed to be a publicity stunt. A hundred years later, it is remembered as far more. In March 1925, Tennessee became the first state in the country to ban the teaching of evolution in public school classrooms.
item.dc:creator: KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fox News ‘Antisemitism Exposed’ Newsletter: Team Trump puts anti-Israel campus ringleader on ICE
item.description: Fox News' "Antisemitism Exposed" newsletter brings you stories on the rising anti-Jewish prejudice across the U.S. and the world.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump threatens Europe with 200 percent tariff on wine, Champagne
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday threatened to put a hefty 200 percent tariff on wine and Champagne coming from Europe if the European Union (EU) does not remove a tariff on whisky.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: 5 risks facing the US economy under Trump
item.description: Warning signs have been flashing and markets have been sliding amid policy uncertainties and some troubling macroeconomic indicators.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, KRON4

item.title: Missing American college student search potentially complicated by power outage: expert
item.description: A power outage at the Punta Cana, Dominican Republic, resort where a missing American college student had been vacationing could potentially impact the investigation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vaccinating poultry could help cut soaring egg prices, but the US is hesitant
item.description: Without a new policy including vaccines, the government will continue to slaughter every flock with a bird flu infection to limit the spread of the disease. Those deaths have totaled over 166 million birds in the U.S. since 2022.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Columbia anti-Israel protest ringleader Mahmoud Khalil to remain detained in Louisiana
item.description: Mahmoud Khalil, one of the ringleaders of anti-Israel protests at Columbia University last year, will remain in ICE custody for now following a hearing in a Manhattan federal court on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A government program made tax filing free and more efficient. Musk and DOGE may get rid of it anyway
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Mia Francis, a 22-year-old barista from Boston, filed her taxes on her own this year for the first time, using a free government tax filing program that made it easy because it did most of the work for her.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Immigrants fuel growth in major US urban counties
item.description: Immigrants kept the largest urban counties in the U.S. growing last year. Core counties in the Houston, Miami and Phoenix metropolitan areas grew more than any others in the country primarily because of people moving in from outside the United States, according to population estimates from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Las Vegas Strip hotel to be redesigned by reality TV star
item.description: Lisa Vanderpump announced Wednesday she plans to completely renovate a Strip hotel, transforming it into the "Vanderpump Hotel.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, ABC 4

item.title: A former Tennessee lawmaker wasn't allowed to reverse his guilty plea. Now, Trump has pardoned him
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — President Donald Trump has pardoned a former Republican Tennessee lawmaker who was two weeks into a 21-month prison sentence for an illegal campaign finance scheme that he pleaded guilty to in 2022, before he tried unsuccessfully to take back his plea. Former Sen.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: USDA ends program that helped schools serve food from local farmers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. Agriculture Department is ending two pandemic-era programs that provided more than $1 billion for schools and food banks to purchase food from local farmers and producers.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Virginia track athlete facing assault charge after baton strike she said was accidental
item.description: Alaila Everett said it was an accident but court records show she is now facing a potential assault and battery charge.
item.dc:creator: Kenzie Finch, KRON4

item.title: Passenger attacked flight attendant, swallowed rosary beads on plane to Miami: court docs
item.description: His sister told the FBI agent that they were traveling to Haiti “to flee religious attacks of a spiritual nature.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 5 men accused of blocking traffic with twerking
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (WREG) -- Five people were arrested Monday night after police say they blocked a downtown intersection with their dancing.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Moon, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The US is short nearly 4 million homes
item.description: Builders faced barriers like zoning and supply costs, which make producing affordable homes challenging.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, ABC 4
